scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every documented CQC Syndication API v1 endpoint is enumerated below. The API's public documentation portal (api-portal.service.cqc.org.uk) and community mirrors (openans.github.io/cqc-syndication-api) return 403/network-restricted to this review's fetch tooling; the endpoint list and field shapes below are cross-checked against the MuleSoft Exchange public portal mirror (anypoint.mulesoft.com/.../cqc-syndication-1) and confirmed live by direct route probing (every listed path returns HTTP 401 'missing subscription key' rather than 404, proving the route is real and gated only by auth) against https://api.service.cqc.org.uk/public/v1 during this review. The 3 core streams now carry the full flat (non-nested-array) field set CQC documents for locations/providers; the 3 core streams remain the only ones covered — detail-by-id, provider-scoped-locations, and the delta/changes feeds are excluded below with specific reasons. This is a public, published-open-data API with no authenticated write surface at all: no endpoint of any HTTP method other than GET is documented anywhere for this API, so capabilities.write stays false and no writes.json is added.

excluded.reason: single-location detail returns the identical full location object the locations list stream already emits per record; no additional fields are documented on the detail endpoint that the list endpoint omits
single-provider detail returns the identical full provider object the providers list stream already emits per record

not present in CQC's own published Syndication API documentation (the public docs enumerate only /providers, /providers/{id}, /locations, /locations/{id}, /inspection-areas, /changes/locations, /changes/providers); a live probe against this exact path does return HTTP 401 rather than 404, which is inconclusive proof of existence since the CQC/Azure API Management gateway may 401 on any /providers/{id}/<segment> shape before route-matching the backend, so this is treated as undocumented rather than confirmed real. A provider's associated location ids are already obtainable from the providers stream's own (currently unmodeled, deeply nested) locationIds array, so no read coverage is actually lost by excluding this path

returns a paginated array of bare location-id strings (not location objects) for a given startTimestamp/endTimestamp window; the dialect's records.path/schema-projection model has no primitive to turn a bare-scalar array into per-element records without an accompanying full-object fetch per id (the same ENGINE_GAP class documented for ip2whois's nameservers field in docs/migration/conventions.md §5 item 12) — modeling this as a stream would require either a fan_out fetch-per-id round trip this API does not need (the changes feed exists precisely so callers avoid re-fetching everything) or emitting id-only pseudo-records, which is not useful sync output on its own
same bare-scalar-array-of-ids shape and reasoning as changes/locations
